page: The Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is a High Performance virtual machine featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU built on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The CPU operates at a base speed of 2.0 GHz, featuring 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded capabilities due to the single-core shared allocation. The instance achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the weak and poor categories, respectively. Memory performance is mixed, with write bandwidth for large blocks performing at an average level, while read-only bandwidth remains weak.

Given the shared CPU allocation and 2.0 GB memory limit, this instance exhibits poor performance in database operations, PassMark physics simulations, and static web serving throughput. It is qualitatively highly cost-efficient for low-intensity, single-threaded tasks, utility scripts, and lightweight development environments. It is not suited for multi-threaded applications, heavy database hosting, or high-throughput web serving.
description: The Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While its single-core performance is highly competitive, ranking in the top tier for stress-ng single-core tests and showing strong single-threaded compression speeds, its multi-core and database performance are heavily constrained by the single shared vCPU allocation. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic utility scripts, and development environments, though it remains poorly suited for resource-intensive database operations, multi-threaded applications, or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is a virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage for budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual instance pairing a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa core with fast local NVMe storage for lightweight tasks.
bullet_points: Single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU running at 2.0 GHz
2.0 GB system memory and 50 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Strong single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression speeds
Weak multi-core, database, and static web serving throughput
Best suited for lightweight utility scripts and development environments
